meta.topic: The future seen from the past

step_1_context.text: There are a number of ways of talking about an activity or event that was in the future at a particular point in the past. In order to express this idea, we can use the past tenses of the verb forms w

step_2_explanation.simple_rule: There are a number of ways of talking about an activity or event that was in the future at a particular point in the past.
step_2_explanation.formula:
To talk about an activity or event that was in the future at a particular point in the past, we can use was / were to + infinitive (for things that actually happened) and was / were to have + past participle (for things that were expected, but didn't happen):
 • At the time she was probably the best
step_2_explanation.common_mistake: Pay attention to the context when choosing the correct form.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 14.1.1 I'm going to do the washing, but we'd run out of washing powder.
options: was going to do ✓ (future from past)
step_3_interactive_exercises.correct_answer: was going to do ✓ (future from past)
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Past point of view: 'I had plans to...' → was going to.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 14.1.2 The concert tonight would be over by about 9:30. We could eat after that.
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: 'Would be over' is correct — future from past perspective.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 14.1.3 When we were passing Ivan's house, we thought we'd drop in and see him.
options: Correct ✓ (past continuous for future from past)
step_3_interactive_exercises.correct_answer: Correct ✓ (past continuous for future from past)
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Past continuous ('were passing') for planned/ongoing future from past — correct.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 14.1.4 A: Sorry, I thought Ella will have shown you. Over there.
options: would have shown ✓ (future perfect from past)
step_3_interactive_exercises.correct_answer: would have shown ✓ (future perfect from past)
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Future perfect from past → would have shown.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 14.1.5 The manager of Newtown United said that the team is to be announced at nine tomorrow.
options: Correct ✓ (still in future for speaker — either form possible)
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Still in future for speaker → both 'is to be' and 'was to be' are possible.
